_comment: Ground truth for M7.mma.2 (stress-constrained optimization). Maintainer-authored. REUSES the committed l-bracket.step geometry (no new geometry; geometry facts live in the existing expected_values.json). This fixture pins the LOAD CASE and the STRESS CAP, and the M7.mma.2 test asserts BEHAVIORAL INVARIANTS (constrained vs unconstrained), NOT an absolute stress value. Rationale below.
_why_no_pinned_stress: The L-bracket's re-entrant inner corner is a geometric stress singularity: in exact elasticity the von Mises there is unbounded, and a voxel FEA's measured peak RISES with resolution and never converges (independent reference measured peak von Mises per unit force of 9.81e-2, 1.149e-1, 1.352e-1 MPa/N at resolutions 24, 32, 48 - monotonically increasing, no plateau). An absolute peak-stress value is therefore a mesh artifact and MUST NOT be pinned as a golden number; it would spuriously fail at any other resolution. This matches the existing fixture policy (see expected_values.json) of pinning geometry exactly but run results as behavioral invariants.
_provenance: Load case, force scaling, and the cap-bites property computed by an independent standalone NumPy/SciPy voxel hex-FEA (shares no code with core/; mma2_reference.py, maintainer-run and verified). Peak stress location at res 48 = (23.1, -0.6, 8.1) mm = the re-entrant inner corner, as physically expected. Provenance rule per DECISIONS.md 2026-07-04.

anchor.description: top face of the upright wall fully clamped (all 3 DOF)

load.description: total downward force distributed over the far plate face (x = -30), plate thickness only (z in [0,8])
load._force_note: Chosen so the full-solid (unconstrained) peak von Mises at res 48 is ~3x PLA yield, i.e. the stress cap is comfortably exceeded and the constraint is genuinely active (KKT). Linear elasticity => stress scales linearly with force; the exact value is not critical, only that the cap bites.

l_bracket_stress_case._stress_cap_note: PLA yield strength (materials.json). The unconstrained full-solid design's peak von Mises at res 48 is ~3x this cap (independent reference: 165 MPa vs 55 MPa cap, i.e. ~3.0x), so a stress-constrained run MUST change the design to satisfy it.

behavioral_invariants.unconstrained_exceeds_cap: an MMA compliance-minimizing run at this load case WITHOUT the stress constraint leaves peak von Mises strictly above stress_cap_mpa (the singular corner guarantees this at any resolution).
behavioral_invariants.constrained_satisfies_cap: the stress-constrained MMA run brings peak von Mises to <= stress_cap_mpa * (1 + tol), where tol is the aggregation slack of the p-norm/KS constraint (record the chosen aggregation + tol in DECISIONS.md per M7.mma.2). Do NOT assert equality to a pinned MPa value.
behavioral_invariants.constraint_costs_compliance: the constrained design's compliance is >= the unconstrained design's compliance at the same volume fraction (satisfying the cap costs stiffness). This is the core proof that the constraint is active and respected.
behavioral_invariants.peak_moves_or_thickens_at_corner: the constrained design retains/adds material at the re-entrant corner region (near x~22, z~8) relative to the unconstrained design (optional structural check; connectivity-based, not pixel-perfect).

_reviewer_sanity_reference.note: NOT asserted by the test - documentation so a reviewer can confirm the case is set up to bite. From the independent reference at res 48:
